reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 14.9% and 20-day momentum is 43.7%.
Trend quality is supported by 50% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 18.3% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 4.5%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.1%.
Volume confirmation is 117.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 13.8% versus SPY over 5 days and 12.7% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.70, confidence 0.44, and 0.6 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.86, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 1.00, average source quality 0.83, confidence 0.80.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.119 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Wolfe Research Raises Texas Instruments (TXN) PT to $315, Truist Lifts Target after Strong Q1
news_evidence.llm_reason: Wolfe Research raising its price target (and Truist lifting its target) after a strong Q1 is direct, timely analyst action that can drive near-term flows and sentiment. This is actionable company-specific news likely to have a positive short-term impact.

news_evidence.title: Texas Instruments Incorporated filed 10-Q: 10-Q
news_evidence.llm_reason: The SEC 10-Q is a material, company-specific filing. If the filing contains surprises (revisions, guidance changes, liabilities), it could be market-moving; absent evidence of surprises, the routine quarterly filing is necessary but typically only modestly impacts the stock in the immediate 1–10 day window.
